feed.title: Sustainable Energy Storage & Recycling
feed.subtitle: Welcome to the Sustainable Energy Storage & Recycling Group, led by [Dr.Georgios Nikiforidis](https://www.ucl.ac.uk/institute-for-materials-discovery/people/dr-georgios-nikiforidis) at the University College London. Our mission is to advance the development and implementation of sustainable energy storage materials and technologies, focusing on the circular economy of electric vehicle (EV) batteries, bio-energy production, and green electrolyte engineering. We are at the forefront of research and development, pioneering solutions that bridge the gap between green energy and cutting-edge digital processes. With a shared commitment to a more sustainable future, we explore the intersection of renewable energy technologies and digital optimization methods. Our expertise and collaborative spirit drive us to create a world where clean energy and digital innovation coexist harmoniously, revolutionizing the way we power our planet. Join us on this journey towards a sustainable future and discover the innovative projects and groundbreaking research being pursued by our dedicated team.

entry.title: Arrival of PhD Students Haotian Ma, Ziwei Chen, and Sijin Liu

link.title: Arrival of PhD Students Haotian Ma, Ziwei Chen, and Sijin Liu

entry.content: We are excited to announce the arrival of new PhD students Haotian Ma, Ziwei Chen, and Sijin Liu to our group. They bring fresh energy and expertise to our ongoing research in electrochemistry. Stay tuned for updates on their contributions and the progress of their work.
entry.summary: We are excited to announce the arrival of new PhD students Haotian Ma, Ziwei Chen, and Sijin Liu to our group. They bring fresh energy and expertise to our ongoing research in electrochemistry. Stay tuned for updates on their contributions and the progress of their work.

entry.title: Group Website Construction
entry.content: A test page for the group website has been constructed, with news section added.
entry.summary: A test page for the group website has been constructed, with news section added.
